key_insight: Subgrain-resolved thermomechanical incompatibility in Cu metallization can localize plastic deformation and identify degradation hotspots during rapid short-circuit-like loading.
engineering_relevance: The study informs reliability assessment and lifetime design of power semiconductor metallization by linking microstructural deformation mechanisms to device-scale degradation risk.
method: Scanning 3DXRD tracks grain- and subgrain-resolved deformation during rapid cyclic loading.
Thermomechanical crystal plasticity simulations reproduce the loading-driven stress and plasticity evolution.
Experiment and simulation are coupled to identify likely hotspot formation mechanisms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports integrated experimental-computational analysis of grain-resolved plastic deformation evolution under rapid cyclic loading, but does not provide quantitative accuracy metrics.
watch_item: Hotspot predictions depend on model fidelity, loading representation, and the limited experimental sampling of microstructural states.
paper_title: Subgrain-resolved Analysis of Degradation in Cu Metallization via Scanning 3DXRD and Thermomechanical Modeling
